## Operations

## RBAC rules

- admin: read public and private agents, update all, delete all
- owner: read owned agents, update owned agents, delete owned agents
- user: read owned agents and other users' public agents only

## Visibility Enum

- `AgentVisibility`: `public`, `private`

## Schemas

- `AgentClientRecord`: `agent_id`, `owner_principal_id`, `is_owned_by_viewer`, `visibility`
- `AgentClientRecordV2`: `agent_id`, `owner_principal_id`, `workspace_id`, `is_owned_by_viewer`, `visibility`
- `ClientBootstrapResponse`: `schema_version`, `workspace_id`, `agents`
- `ClientBootstrapResponseV2`: `schema_version`, `workspace_id`, `agents`
- `AgentClientListResponse`: `schema_version`, `agents`
- `AgentClientListResponseV2`: `schema_version`, `agents`

## Invariant anchors

- admin may read public and private agents and may update or delete all agents.
- owner may read, update, and delete owned agents.
- user may read owned agents and other users' public agents only.
- AgentVisibility is a closed public/private enum in generated client contracts.

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: Admin/owner/public/private visibility vocabulary lived in policy prose while DSL policy, schemas, enum, and query operations already defined the machine-readable contract.
- Hypothesis: A focused visibility verifier can make RBAC vocabulary drift observable without expanding mutation-safety or thumbnail scope.
- Execute: Verify RBAC policy rules, visibility enum values, visible-agent response schemas, RBAC-bound query operations, DSL/IR tuple parity, and OpenAPI projection.
- Evaluate: CI fails when RBAC rules, public/private enum values, schema identity fields, generated reader, or v1/v2 visibility query projections drift.
- Retrospective: Future visibility semantics should update API policy/schema fixtures and this manifest before reader text changes.
